payload.text:
Adicione também a opção de pagamento com PayPal e cartão

💰 Sugestão de Planos (ótima para conversão e lucro)

🟦 Plano Starter – R$ 37 a R$ 97/mês

Para iniciantes e pequenos negócios.
Inclui:

Geração limitada de criativos (ex: 50/mês)

Geração limitada de copies

5 vídeos curtos /mês

Biblioteca de referências do setor

Sem análise profunda


🎯 Objetivo: ser acessível e converter muitos usuários.


---

🟨 Plano Pro – R$ 97 a R$ 197/mês (plano principal)

Para afiliados, gestores de tráfego e pequenas agências.
Inclui:

Geração ilimitada de criativos

30–50 vídeos / mês

Copy ilimitada

Analisador de anúncios avançado

Planejador de campanha

Brand Kit IA

Suporte prioridade


🎯 Este deve ser o seu plano mais vendido (plano de “melhor custo-benefício”).


---

🟧 Plano Agency – R$ 197 a R$ 497/mês

Para agências, infoprodutores grandes e e-commerces.
Inclui:

Uso 100% ilimitado

150+ vídeos/mês

Time workspace (até 5 usuários)

Exportação em massa

Pastas da marca avançadas

Insights do setor em tempo real

Licença comercial estendida


🎯 Sua maior fonte de lucro.


---

🟥 Plano Enterprise – R$ 597+ /mês

Para empresas grandes, redes de franquias e e-commerces robustos.
Inclui:

IA personalizada para o setor

Treinamento com base nos anúncios próprios do cliente

Usuários ilimitados

Conta manager

APIs

Integrações avançadas


🎯 Poucos clientes, lucro absurdo por cada um.


---

📌 Resumo rápido para você escolher rápido

Se quiser começar simples:

R$ 37 → Plano Básico
R$ 97 → Plano Pro
R$ 297 → Plano Agency

**Initiating the Analysis**

I'm starting the thought process by breaking down the user request. Their goal is clear: integrate billing or subscription functionality into AdZo. My initial focus is on pinpointing the specific requirements, like payment options (PayPal and credit cards). It's a fundamental step to map out the scope.


**Developing Pricing Structures**

I've been working on defining the pricing tiers for the subscription model. I've landed on Starter, Pro, Agency, and Enterprise plans, each with defined monthly price points and feature inclusions like video limits and access to advanced tools. I'm focusing on user needs and how to offer options which are appealing and sustainable. I need to make these choices clear and easy to find for the user.


**Constructing the Interface**

I'm now in the process of building the user interface, starting with the plan cards within the subscription component. I'm focusing on monthly vs yearly options, plan descriptions, and clear calls to action. The look and feel is progressing to create intuitive actions for the user. I'm aiming for simplicity and ease of use.


**Visualizing Payment Flow**

I'm currently picturing the user flow for payments. Clicking a plan button triggers a modal with "Cartão de Crédito" and "PayPal" tabs. The card tab shows a form for number, expiry, CVC, and name, while the PayPal tab has a "Pagar com PayPal" button. A summary of the selected plan and price will be present. I also need to make updates to the app and sidebar components to link to the subscription functionality I'm creating.

**Defining Subscription Structure**

I've been working on defining the pricing tiers. The tiers include Starter, Pro, Agency, and Enterprise. I also need to provide clear limits and features for each plan, such as video limits, users per account, and AI tools for each. I'm focusing on user needs and sustainable options.


**Implementing the Components**

I'm now deep in the implementation. I've successfully added the new subscription item to the sidebar with the icon. The app component is updated to route to the subscription area. The `Subscription` component is the main focus, and I've structured it with `useState` hooks for plan selection, billing cycle, modal state, and payment method. I'm focusing on rendering plan cards and implementing the checkout modal.


**Finalizing UI Design**

I'm now in the final stages of the UI. I'm focusing on aesthetics, ensuring the pricing page looks clean and modern using Tailwind, with gradients and clear typography, while highlighting the "Pro" plan. Functionality is working, with toggles and modal buttons. I've switched language to Portuguese. Although Gemini API isn't directly involved in the billing page, I have checked all imports to ensure no conflicts occur. I've integrated `lucide-react` for icons. I decided to make the Enterprise plan a full-width banner. I made sure the modal shows the user requested options. The layout is optimized to fit all four plans nicely.

Here are the changes to add the Subscription plans, payment options (Credit Card & PayPal), and the necessary navigation updates.

I will create a new component `Subscription.tsx` that implements the pricing tiers you described (Starter, Pro, Agency, Enterprise) and includes a mock checkout modal with payment method selection.

entries.description: Create new Subscription component with Pricing Plans and Payment Modal (Credit Card/PayPal).
Add Subscription item to sidebar menu.
Add Subscription route to switch statement.
